text: Board House Coffee — WHEN: Sat @11:30am PARKING: Since it's in an outdoor retail center, they have a shared lot with free parking, but I would imagine it being limited. SEATINGS: + About 5 tables that seat 2 outside along the sidewalk/walkway. + 1 Counter facing out against the window that seats 2-3 + 5 Tables indoors that seat 1-2 + 1 Large tables that seats up to 6 + 1 cozy chair in the corner with a tiny coffee table for 1 I see outlets along the wall and under the counter seating sounder top. Such a great place to work, and they don't play their lovely music selection too loud. Also there are single panel blinds along all the windows for us to adjust if it gets too sunny...so thoughtful! Very chill vibe, mom n pops kind of feel. Nothing fancy, but you know what, it provides everything we need. They serve all coffee beverages, smoothies, tea, snacks, pastries, and breakfast and full lunch meals! Oh, and they even have a printer here if you need something printed! For just $0.85 more you can upgrade to a large (I wish this was listed directly beneath the coffee listed on the menu). But I can always get a refill for only $1.75. Apologies but I'm not a coffee connoisseur so I can't tell you if it's good or not. I just need the caffeine and my admission to stay and work. WiFi - I respect there being Wi-Fi available only for paying customers. As in, the Wi-Fi password is on the bottom of your receipt. I found mind in the link of my email receipt! Not hard to find! The staff seem quite friendly and very hardworking. You can smell and see all the goods they are cooking up behind the counter. I can totally see myself coming back to to get some work done with the option of food when I get hungry! THIS SPOT PROVIDES!!!

text: Bonsai Coffee Bar — I tried out this cute café in Sawtelle with my roommate while studying for midterms, and overall, I thought it was pretty good. The interior and decor are super cute, and there are plenty of seats available. However, it does get pretty busy, so spots fill up quickly. The noise level is generally manageable since most people are working, but it can occasionally get crowded and loud. I usually wear noise-canceling AirPods, so it didn't bother me much. The baristas are also really sweet and kind whenever I interact with them. I ordered the matcha latte, and I'd say it was pretty good. Definitely not the best I've had, but also far from the worst, and probably one of the better ones in the area that I've tried. The price was pretty average for L.A. and the cup size was decent enough to last me through about five hours of studying there. The only downside is that the free Wi-Fi only lasts for two hours. After that, I had to switch to my phone hotspot, so I'd recommend bringing a power bank if you plan on staying for a while. When logging into the Wi-Fi, it asks for a phone number, so I'm not sure if it tracks access by device IP or phone number. Also for my people taking the bus back to UCLA, note that there is no visible bus sign due to construction in the area. It's really confusing at first and you will get scared, but my tip is to wait with a small group of people around where the sign should be and usually the bus will stop (sometimes it doesn't though unfortunately). Overall, this is a really cute and nice café for getting work done or doing some studying as long as you don't mind the time-limited wifi. Matcha Latte Helpful

text: Motoring Coffee — Café with one of the best ambiances, but the drink and food item I had was just okay. I visited this coffee shop on an early Tuesday morning before work and my experience here was good. For drinks, I got their specialty drink "Traction Control" Iced which is essentially a blueberry matcha drink. It was okay, but I've had better matcha blueberry drinks. I also wasn't the biggest fan of the flavor and the grainy bits. For food I got their ham and cheese croissant and it was good, but not worth the $7.50 paid for it. It is quite big though. They warm it up upon request and it was nicely flaky. I also appreciated how the barista gave me a thorough rundown of the coffee shop's specialties. The best part of this spot is the ambiance. The interior is big and full of different vehicles. It was like a motor vehicle museum. There are also lots of table and chairs scattered around and even some comfy seating areas. There's also Wi-Fi, but I didn't see any outlets. This is a great place to relax, socialize, and get some work done. I also like how there's a free photo print machine. Super cute! Overall, a generally good experience here. I would come back for the ambiance alone, but would order different things.

text: Motoring Coffee — You know it really says something when a Teriyaki vendor for an event can't sell because the clientele at this cafe are the types not to eat rice.. I'm not a car person so I can't comment too much on that but I visited because there was an event and for an event space it's pretty neat. Lots of vintage vehicles and memorabilia for photo ops in the main area and they have their own merch and members' club as well. There's a driving simulator, lots of spaces for vendors to post up and sell their wares, a garage with lots of fancy cars in the back, a cozy lounge area with empanadas and alcohol being sold, and of course you can get coffee here too. Since there was a line due to the event, it almost went out the door and took awhile to get our drinks despite the multiple baristas working. Probably just fine on a normal day though. Parking was limited but we managed to score street parking. I got a London Fog Latte and it was super bland and watered down despite making no adjustments to it. So I guess you yourself have to add sugar and syrup. I'd had better at Starbucks and even Gong Cha for god sake's so as an actual coffee place it's pretty mid. Cool cars and display though!

INDOOR + OUTDOOR SEATS Indoor sofas, tables, seats and bar seats. Outdoor tables and seats available. Very relaxed vibe overall. text: The Boy & The Bear — I'd really wanna give this spot a 3 start but don't wanna bring this place down. I haven written a review in a while but after coming here I needed to share my frustration with the shop. The place is so beautiful and it's such a vibe to just be there. I am amazed at how much detail went to the shop. The couches are nice and the staff is friendly. My problem is with the coffee. I was left wanting a different flavor than I expected. For context it was a Sunday morning so it might've been busy. But I ordered a flat white and it tasted like filtered water with a dash of coffee. There was absolutely no flavor and could barely taste the coffee. Maybe I don't know coffee well but I've been to tons of coffee shops in the west side and this is for sure one of the ones I was disappointed by. I'm willing to give the place a second chance but not sure.

text: The Boy & The Bear — A bit disappointed in this place. First off, they're running a promotion that doesn't work. I signed up for texts and went to use the code on my first order and online order, and it wouldn't take it. Second, the cold brew was good but not as strong as I like it (that's based on taste), but the matcha was disappointing. They hyped it up in the menu, but it was pretty weak. Too much milk and not enough matcha. I've had match a lot lately so it was noticeably milky.

text: The Boy & The Bear — Such a fun coffee shop to visit on a weekend and relax with a book. There's widow-facing seating as well as a couch, making for a sweet cozy homely vibe. The menu is simple, I like to order a cappuccino. The milk options are whole, oat, or almond (as seen on the menu). Your cup of coffee or matcha can get pretty pricy the more you modify!

text: The Boy & The Bear — Really tasty coffee & great recommendations by our barista! I got an iced salted caramel latte of sorts and my friend got a regular iced latte. It was perfect- not too sweet, and not acidic. Will def come again. We parked on the street during the daytime. Seems like a good hidden little spot to hole up. The yuzu almond croissant I shared w my friend was equally tasty, though on the lighter flavour profile of yuzu- more almond. Still, would def get again

text: The Boy & The Bear — This place is for people who KNOW coffee. I brought my cousin here to try the coffee out! She looks for places that serve single origin. She got into a great conversation with the barista. The barista definitely knows his stuff. They were comparing different coffee shops and discussing the different notes in their beans. My cousin takes her coffee seriously and she was really impressed! I myself went with a flat white. You can definitely tell that their coffee quality is top notch. What I really do appreciate is the fact that their baristas is so knowledgable and really does have a passion for coffee. My cousin said it's not very common to find in coffee shops. She gave me a whole ted talk on why the coffee here is really good. Definitely come here if you want some good quality coffee and a barista who is happy to chat about it!

text: The Boy & The Bear — Stopped by on my way to work and had a nice experience. I ordered at the counter and tried the matcha latte with oat milk. It was okay, I'd try something different next time if I'd go back. The almond croissant was fresh and flaky. I spend about $16 for both. Service was friendly, and the space is inviting with couches and tables along the window, great for chatting or getting some work done. Pastry selection was limited and parking can be tough since it's on the busy main street on Washington. It's meter parking. I got lucky and found parking in front, but can see it being challenging during peak times.
